title: [FEATURE] WaterCrawl blocked by Cloudflare
author: sekedus
body: ## Problem Statement WaterCrawl fails to crawl URLs protected by Cloudflare, such as `https://imginn.com/instagram/`. The engine feedback shows a **Playwright request failed (Forbidden)** and an **exception handling error** (`exceptions must derive from BaseException`). This prevents successful data retrieval from Cloudflare‑guarded sites. ## Proposed Solution Integrate [Scrapling](https://github.com/d4vinci/Scrapling) as an optional crawling backend. Scrapling provides built‑in Cloudflare bypass capabilities and can handle dynamic content rendering more reliably than Playwright alone. Allow users to select Scrapling as an alternative engine when encountering Cloudflare blocks. ## Alternative Solutions - Use rotating proxies or headless browser fingerprinting to mimic real user behavior. - Implement retry logic with different user agents and session cookies. - Employ external services like Cloudflare‑bypass APIs (if licensing permits). ## User Impact This feature would enable WaterCrawl to access and extract data from Cloudflare‑protected sites, improving coverage and reliability for users who need to crawl modern web pages with anti‑bot protection. ## Implementation Details - Add Scrapling as a pluggable engine in the crawler architecture. - Detect Cloudflare challenge pages automatically and switch to Scrapling when necessary. - Log fallback events for transparency and debugging. - Ensure compatibility with existing Playwright‑based workflows. ## Mockups Refer to the attached screenshot showing the **“Forbidden”** and **BaseException** errors during crawl attempts. ## Additional Context The issue occurred while crawling `https://imginn.com/instagram/` at 12:56 PM, resulting in zero items retrieved. This enhancement would prevent similar failures and improve resilience against Cloudflare restrictions.

title: [FEATURE] Download of PDF, Docx files linked on html sites
author: ramspe
body:
## Problem Statement
When i scrape official websites that we need documentation from, often they have links to pdf document(like manuals) or other .docx files and similar, and would like them to come with the crawl.

## Proposed Solution
Add Page Options under Crawl Playground that lets you download documents available on the website.

## User Impact
Deeper and more "complete" scraping.

## Implementation Details
No clue.

## Mockups
Add another box, perhaps under "Actions" that would be a on/off for "Include documents"

## Additional Context
:)

title: Remediate Dependabot security alerts by upgrading minimatch, axios, and webpack in frontend and docs builds
author: qodo-code-review[bot]
body:
**Description:**
  - Dependabot has identified additional vulnerable JavaScript dependencies after the earlier package upgrade effort, requiring follow-up remediation.
  - The documentation site build and frontend dependency trees include versions of `minimatch`, `axios`, and `webpack` with known DoS/SSRF/ReDoS vulnerabilities.
  - The project needs deterministic, repo-wide enforcement of patched dependency versions, including for transitive dependencies, to reduce supply-chain risk.
  - Security upgrades must not break local development or documentation build workflows.

**Deliverables:**
  - **Upgrade axios:** Update the frontend’s direct `axios` dependency to a patched version (>= `1.13.5`) and ensure any transitive `axios` usage is also forced to patched versions.
  - **Harden minimatch:** Enforce a patched `minimatch` version (>= `10.2.1`) across both `frontend/` and `docs/`, including transitive dependency resolution.
  - **Patch webpack:** Ensure the docs build resolves `webpack` to a non-vulnerable version (>= `5.104.1`) to address reported SSRF-related advisories.
  - **Lockfile integrity:** Regenerate and commit updated pnpm lockfiles (`frontend/pnpm-lock.yaml`, `docs/pnpm-lock.yaml`) so dependency resolution is consistent in CI and for developers.
  - **Regression verification:** Confirm `pnpm install` and core dev workflows (frontend dev server and docs build/dev) run successfully after dependency upgrades.

title: Patch known dependency vulnerabilities and improve Scrapy failure observability with Sentry
author: qodo-code-review[bot]
body:
**Description:**
  - Security scanners are reporting high/moderate vulnerabilities in backend, frontend, and docs dependency trees that require remediation to reduce exploit risk.
  - Current Scrapy execution and spider lifecycle failures are not reliably captured in Sentry, limiting operational visibility and slowing incident triage.
  - When Scrapy subprocesses fail, stdout/stderr are not persisted in logs, making it difficult to diagnose crawling failures.
  - Some configuration examples contain typos (e.g., MinIO secret key) that can lead to misconfiguration and support overhead.

**Deliverables:**
  - **Dependency patching:** Upgrade/pin affected backend dependencies (e.g., `django`, `urllib3`, `cryptography`, `sqlparse`) to non-vulnerable versions while maintaining compatibility (including keeping `scrapy` on a stable compatible release).
  - **Frontend/docs overrides:** Add package manager overrides in `frontend` and `docs` to force patched transitive versions for known vulnerable JS packages (e.g., `react-router-dom`, `fast-xml-parser`, `lodash`, `qs`, `ajv`, `diff`, `@isaacs/brace-expansion`).
  - **Sentry spider errors:** Ensure unhandled Scrapy spider runtime errors are reported to Sentry via a Scrapy extension using `signals.spider_error`, while ignoring expected/control-flow/network noise exceptions.
  - **Init error capture:** Ensure spider initialization failures (e.g., exceptions thrown during `from_crawler`) are captured and flushed to Sentry to avoid silent startup failures.
  - **Failure diagnostics:** When Scrapy subprocess runs fail, capture and log stdout/stderr and mark related crawl/search/sitemap requests as failed with duration updates; avoid attempting Sentry event creation when the Sentry client is inactive.

title: Update manual version bump workflow to synchronize documentation version references
author: qodo-code-review[bot]
body:
**Description:**
  - Manual release version bumps currently update code and deployment files but miss version references in self-hosting documentation.
  - Outdated versions in docs can confuse users and lead to incorrect self-hosted configurations.
  - The workflow should automatically propagate the new release version to relevant documentation files during a manual bump.
  - This ensures consistency across Docker images, environment examples, and documented configuration instructions.

**Deliverables:**
  - **Docs coverage:** Extend the manual version bump workflow to update version strings in `docs/docs/self-hosted/configuration.md` and `docs/docs/self-hosted/services.md`.
  - **Accurate patterns:** Match and replace semantic version patterns (e.g., `vX.Y.Z`) for documented variables (e.g., backticked `VERSION`, `VERSION=` examples) and image tags (`watercrawl/watercrawl:`, `watercrawl/frontend:`).
  - **Commit summary:** Include updated documentation files in the commit output and PR body generated by the workflow.
  - **Graceful handling:** Add existence checks and warnings if target documentation files are missing without failing the workflow.

title: Add proxy server configuration and UI for sitemap requests with team-based access controls
author: qodo-code-review[bot]
body:
**Description:**
  - Sitemap generation must support routing requests through configurable proxy servers to improve reliability, geo-targeting, and compliance needs.
  - Teams need to select from their available proxies; selection should be validated so only team-accessible proxies can be used.
  - The UI should allow users to choose a proxy for a sitemap task, with visibility and selection constrained by subscription (e.g., premium proxies restricted for default/free plans).
  - Proxy settings must propagate through the sitemap crawling flow (robots.txt, sitemap fetch, HTML crawl) while excluding Google Custom Search calls from proxying.
  - System-wide limits and settings (e.g., Google search pagination) should be configurable via environment variables and surfaced in spider settings.

**Deliverables:**
  - Proxy option field: Add `proxy_server` to sitemap options.
  - Team proxy validation: Ensure selected proxy exists within team-accessible proxies; reject invalid slugs.
  - End-to-end proxy usage: Apply selected proxy across sitemap requests and downstream spider requests; exclude Google search API calls from proxy.
  - Frontend proxy selector: Provide a combobox to choose team proxies, with loading state, team filtering, and subscription-based premium restrictions.
  - Config controls: Introduce configurable `SCRAPY_GOOGLE_SEARCH_PAGE_LIMIT` and integrate into spider behavior.

title: Standardize frontend UI with shared components, unified modal system, and consolidated API documentation
author: qodo-code-review[bot]
body:
**Description:**
  - The frontend lacks consistent UI patterns, resulting in duplicated code, inconsistent modals, and disparate page headers/navigation.
  - API documentation examples are scattered per feature with repeated logic and inconsistent language snippets.
  - Design tokens and dark/light mode usage are inconsistent, with hardcoded colors and ad-hoc spacings affecting accessibility and maintainability.
  - Internationalization coverage is incomplete for new/updated UI, and some admin copy is missing across locales.
  - Observability and version exposure should align across frontend and backend (Sentry configuration, API version in UI and OpenAPI).

**Deliverables:**
  - **Shared Modals:** Introduce a reusable `Modal` component and migrate all dialogs (e.g., ProxyForm, ProviderConfigForm, TeamSelector, Result/About/Sitemap modals) to the new standard with documented usage.
  - **API Docs System:** Create a shared `ApiDocumentation` component and `useApiDocumentation` hook; add per-domain implementations (Crawl, Search, Knowledge Base, Sitemap) and code formatters for Python/Node.js/Go snippets.
  - **Layout Unification:** Extract a reusable `Sidebar` with mobile/desktop containers and a `PageHeader` component; apply across dashboard/admin layouts and pages to ensure consistent navigation and headers.
  - **Design Tokens:** Replace hardcoded colors with semantic tokens, refine light/dark themes, and standardize paddings and states; ensure components use tokens for borders, backgrounds, and accents.
  - **i18n Coverage:** Update translation keys and add missing admin descriptions across all supported locales; ensure new shared components use i18next.
  - **UX Enhancements:** Improve empty/loading states and table/list visuals for pages like API Keys and Usage History, maintaining responsive behavior.
  - **Observability/Versioning:** Ensure Sentry initialization respects runtime config/env and surface backend version consistently (OpenAPI `VERSION` from backend `__version__`, UI About modal/version display).

title: [FEATURE] Convert Relative Paths to Absolute Paths for Images When Generating Markdown Files
author: albertlinux
body: ## Problem Statement Hello! First, thank you for developing the watercrawl project. I have a feature suggestion to improve its integration with knowledge bases and AI models. Currently, when watercrawl processes HTML containing images with relative paths (e.g., ), the generated Markdown file retains these relative paths. However, if this Markdown is imported into a knowledge base (e.g., for use with large language models), the relative paths cause broken image links. This prevents AI models from correctly referencing or displaying the images when generating answers. ## Proposed Solution Could you add an option to convert relative image paths to absolute paths during the Markdown generation process? For instance, a relative path like ../images/gk1.jpgwould be resolved to a full URL like https://example.com/images/gk1.jpg based on the source HTML's base URL. This would ensure portability and accuracy when the Markdown is used in external systems ## User Impact AI Compatibility:​ Knowledge bases relying on Markdown input (e.g., for RAG systems) require absolute paths to resolve images correctly, enabling models to provide valid image links in responses. Best Practices:​ Using absolute paths enhances cross-platform consistency, as relative paths can break when files are moved or accessed from different environments. ## Implementation Details This feature could be implemented similarly to tools like Jsoup/URI parsing for path resolution. If feasible, please consider this for a future update! Thank you.

title: Improve social auth resilience: handle missing profile fields and GitHub OAuth error responses
author: qodo-code-review[bot]
body:
**Description:**
  - Social login flows can return incomplete profile data (e.g., missing `given_name` or `family_name`) causing runtime errors.
  - GitHub OAuth may return a 200 status with an error payload or without an `access_token`, leading to exceptions and failed sign-ins.
  - Current implementation assumes presence of specific fields and token, resulting in KeyErrors and unreliable authentication.
  - We need to make social authentication robust to upstream API variations to reduce auth failures and Sentry noise.

**Deliverables:**
  - Graceful name handling: Use optional access for profile name fields.
  - GitHub error detection: Treat responses containing `error` as authentication failures.
  - Access token validation: Verify `access_token` exists before proceeding with GitHub user email lookup.
  - Safe fallback behavior: Return a non-authenticated result (e.g., `None`) on upstream request or payload issues without raising.

title: Upgrade Django framework to version 5.2.8
author: qodo-code-review[bot]
body:
**Description:**
  - Our backend currently pins Django to version 5.2.7 in `backend/pyproject.toml`.
  - A new patch release (5.2.8) is available with security and bug fixes that we should adopt promptly.
  - Keeping the framework updated reduces security risk and ensures compatibility with supported dependencies.
  - The upgrade should be minimal-risk as it is a patch within the same minor version.

**Deliverables:**
  - Version bump update: Update Django pin to 5.2.8 in `backend/pyproject.toml`.
  - Compatibility verification: Confirm application starts, migrations load, and basic flows function under 5.2.8.
  - Security confirmation: Validate that the update addresses known advisories relevant to our version.
  - Documentation note: Record the dependency change in release notes/changelog.

title: Initialize Sentry monitoring across all application entry points
author: qodo-code-review[bot]
body:
**Description:**
  - Unhandled errors are not consistently captured across different runtimes (WSGI app, Django management commands, Scrapy spiders).
  - Lack of centralized error monitoring hinders visibility into production issues and operational failures.
  - Sentry must be initialized early in each process to ensure exceptions and performance data are reported.
  - Initialization should be non-intrusive and safe in all environments, leveraging a single `init_sentry` utility.

**Deliverables:**
  - Centralized init utility: Provide a reusable `init_sentry` initializer accessible across the codebase.
  - WSGI integration: Initialize Sentry in the Django WSGI application startup for production traffic.
  - Management commands: Initialize Sentry in `manage.py` to capture errors from CLI/cron jobs.
  - Scrapy spiders: Initialize Sentry in the spider `settings.py` to monitor crawler executions.
  - Safe initialization: Ensure initialization is idempotent and respects environment/config (e.g., only active when configured).

title: [FEATURE]  Implement Extract API with Prompt-Based URL Discovery and Data Extraction Using LLM
author: amirasaran
body:
## Problem Statement
We need an API to allow users to provide a natural language prompt for data extraction from relevant URLs. The system should identify target URLs and an appropriate JSON schema based on the prompt, then extract the requested information by crawling those URLs and leveraging LLMs. Current challenges include managing model selection, prompt design, and ensuring smooth asynchronous processing and real-time feedback.

## Proposed Solution
Implement two APIs as part of the extraction workflow:

1. **Discovery API:**  
   - Accepts a user prompt.  
   - Uses an LLM to interpret the prompt, discover relevant URLs, and select the best matching JSON schema for data extraction.  
   - Returns a list of URLs, an enhanced version of the user prompt, and the JSON schema.  

2. **Extraction API:**  
   - Takes the list of URLs, enhanced prompt, and JSON schema as inputs.  
   - Runs an asynchronous background task (via Celery) to crawl URLs and extract the requested data using an LLM/agent.  
   - Provides a status endpoint and supports SSE (Server-Sent Events) for real-time progress updates and results streaming.  

Key considerations:  
- Define how to specify and switch between LLM models — globally configured, per user, or API key based.  
- Design effective prompt engineering strategies to improve extraction accuracy.  
- Follow the existing multi-step extraction workflow (create request → run async job → call LLM/agent → return results).  
- Leverage sitemap and search functionalities to enhance URL discovery.  

## Alternative Solutions
- Manual URL input by users instead of automated discovery.  
- Use a fixed extraction schema without prompt-driven schema selection.  
- Run extraction synchronously, but this risks long API response times and poor user experience.

## User Impact
Users gain a powerful, flexible API to perform intelligent, prompt-driven data extraction from relevant websites without manual configuration. Real-time feedback and asynchronous processing improve responsiveness and usability.

## Implementation Details
- Discovery API endpoint to receive prompt, call LLM, and return URLs + enhanced prompt + JSON schema.  
- Extraction API endpoint to accept extraction job requests and enqueue Celery tasks.  
- Status endpoint and SSE endpoint for job updates.  
- Model selection logic configurable globally and/or per user/api key; design API parameters accordingly.  
- Use existing crawling and LLM invocation infrastructure, adapting as needed for prompt-based schema extraction.  
- Incorporate sitemap and search modules for URL discovery enhancement.  

## Mockups
Not provided.

## Additional Context
This feature aligns with existing asynchronous crawling and extraction flows but adds an intelligent, prompt-driven discovery and extraction layer powered by LLMs.

title: [FEATURE] Integrate WaterCrawl Document Loader for Langchain (Node.js)
author: amirasaran
body:
## Problem Statement
Langchain is widely used to build agents in Node.js environments. There is currently no official WaterCrawl document loader that integrates crawling, sitemap, and search functionalities into Langchain for Node.js.

## Proposed Solution
Develop a WaterCrawl document loader for Langchain in Node.js that:
- Uses the official `watercrawl/nodejs` library.
- Supports crawling/scraping, sitemap extraction, and search features.
- Can be directly plugged into Langchain’s document loading pipeline.
- Matches Langchain’s Node.js loader interface standards.

## Alternative Solutions
- Manual integration using HTTP requests and custom code, which is inefficient and error-prone.
- Using generic loaders that do not fully leverage WaterCrawl capabilities.

## User Impact
Node.js developers working with Langchain will be able to directly and efficiently load documents from WaterCrawl, improving data ingestion workflows.

## Implementation Details
- Reference WaterCrawl Node.js client docs: https://docs.watercrawl.dev/clients/nodejs
- Implement a Langchain-compatible DocumentLoader class or module.
- Support configurable modes: crawl, sitemap, and search.
- Provide example usage and automated tests.

## Mockups
Not applicable.

## Additional Context
This will enable the Node.js Langchain ecosystem to take advantage of WaterCrawl’s advanced web data extraction features.

title: [FEATURE] Integrate WaterCrawl Document Loader for Langchain (Python)
author: amirasaran
body:
## Problem Statement
Langchain is a popular framework to connect to LLMs and build agents in Python. To enhance Langchain’s capabilities, we need a document loader that integrates WaterCrawl’s crawling, sitemap, and search features. Currently, there is no native support for WaterCrawl in Langchain’s ecosystem.

## Proposed Solution
Create a WaterCrawl document loader for Langchain in Python that:
- Uses the official `watercrawl-py` library.
- Supports crawling/scraping, sitemap extraction, and search functionalities.
- Can be easily plugged into Langchain’s document loading pipeline.
- Follows Langchain conventions for document loaders.

## Alternative Solutions
- Manually fetch data from WaterCrawl and convert it, but this adds overhead and is less maintainable.
- Use generic HTTP loaders, but they won’t leverage WaterCrawl’s advanced features.

## User Impact
Python developers using Langchain will be able to seamlessly load web documents via WaterCrawl, enhancing data freshness and scope without custom coding.

## Implementation Details
- Reference WaterCrawl Python client docs: https://docs.watercrawl.dev/clients/python
- Implement a Langchain-compatible DocumentLoader class wrapping WaterCrawl features.
- Support parameters for crawl, sitemap, and search modes.
- Include usage examples and tests.

## Mockups
Not applicable.

## Additional Context
This integration will position WaterCrawl as a first-class data source for Langchain in Python.

title: [FEATURE] Embed Interactive API Documentation in Panel
author: amirasaran
body:
## Problem Statement
Currently, users must leave the panel to access the latest API documentation, making it less convenient to explore and test API endpoints. The backend already provides an OpenAPI JSON schema at `/api/schema/team`, but there is no integrated way to view it within the panel.

## Proposed Solution
Integrate an interactive API documentation viewer directly inside the panel, powered by a well-known React OpenAPI rendering library. This should:
- Automatically load the latest OpenAPI schema from `/api/schema/team`.
- Allow users to select which API key to use for testing requests.
- Prevent sending the current panel authentication token; only the selected API key should be used when making calls.
- If the chosen library supports it, enable “try it out” functionality for API calls.

## Alternative Solutions
- Link to an external Swagger UI or Redoc instance, but this would still require users to leave the panel.
- Generate static API documentation within the panel, though it would require manual updates when the API changes.

## User Impact
Developers and technical users will have direct, up-to-date access to the API documentation without leaving the panel, improving productivity and reducing context switching.

## Implementation Details
- Use a popular React OpenAPI rendering library (e.g., Swagger UI React or Redocly).
- Fetch OpenAPI JSON from `/api/schema/team` dynamically.
- Implement API key selection in the UI for authenticated API testing.
- Ensure that panel authentication tokens are not sent to the API when testing; only use the selected API key.
- Keep the integration fully dynamic so no manual changes are required when the API schema updates.

## Mockups
Not provided.

## Additional Context
By leveraging a proven OpenAPI rendering solution, we ensure the documentation remains always in sync with the backend API while providing an improved developer experience.

title: [FEATURE] Allow Cancelling Pending User Invitations
author: amirasaran
body:
## Problem Statement
Currently, once an invitation is sent to a user, there is no way to cancel it. If an incorrect email address is entered or an invitation is sent to the wrong person, the invitation remains active until it is accepted, which can lead to security concerns or confusion.

## Proposed Solution
Introduce a "Cancel Invitation" feature in the panel that allows administrators to revoke pending invitations. Once cancelled, the invited user should no longer be able to accept the invitation, and the invitation should be removed from the pending list.

## Alternative Solutions
- Allow editing the invited email address before it is accepted (though this may introduce complexity).
- Automatically expire invitations after a very short period, but this still doesn’t solve the need for manual cancellation.

## User Impact
Administrators will have better control over invitations, reducing the risk of unauthorized access and improving overall user management efficiency.

## Implementation Details
- Add a "Cancel" button next to each pending invitation in the UI.
- Backend endpoint to handle cancellation requests.
- Update database to mark the invitation as cancelled (with timestamp) or delete the invitation row.
- Ensure the system checks for the "cancelled" status before allowing acceptance if implementing the canceled status.

## Mockups
Not provided.

## Additional Context
This feature is common in user management systems and helps prevent accidental invitations from granting access to unintended users.
